verdict_reasoning: BAX is a soluble cytoplasmic protein at baseline, with its TM domain (α9 helix) sequestered in its own hydrophobic groove, preventing membrane insertion. Upon apoptotic signalling it translocates exclusively to the outer mitochondrial membrane (OMM), where it oligomerizes to form pores. It has no documented stable pool at the plasma membrane outer leaflet under any condition — no GPI anchor, no PM-targeting TM domain, no stable PM TM-partner anchoring, and no reported cancer-cell or stress-induced PM ecto-display. All therapeutic programs (BH3 mimetics, small-molecule BAX activators/inhibitors) engage the intracellular cytosolic or mitochondrial pool. No surface-directed antibody, ADC, or CAR-T program exists for BAX. The protein body is strictly intracellular.
